revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki)]][[ja:甲山1号墳 (岡崎市)]][[zh:甲山1號墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki)]][[ja:甲山1号墳 (岡崎市)]][[zh:甲山1號墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki)]][[ja:甲山1号墳 (岡崎市)]][[zh:甲山1號墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:甲山1号墳 (岡崎市)]][[zh:甲山1號墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.963081|N|137.16635|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}}}
'''Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun''' (甲山1号墳, かぶとやまいちごうふん, also known as 甲山古墳/甲山第1号墳) is an ancient Kofun (kofun) located in {{ill|Rokku, Okazaki|ja|六供町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokku, Okazaki|1=Rokku-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17215518}}, {{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|en|Okazaki, Aichi|qid=Q242783|lt=Okazaki|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. The mound, which is part of the {{ill|Kabutoyama Kofun Group|ja|甲山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, has been designated as a Municipal Historic Site by the city of Okazaki under the name "Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun."
If considered a keyhole-shaped tumulus (zenpō-kōen-fun), it is regarded as the largest kofun in the Mikawa region (central and eastern Aichi Prefecture), and its construction is estimated to date back to the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}, corresponding to the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
== Overview == [[File:Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki), zenkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|No. 1 Kofun (right) and No. 2 Kofun (left)}}]] [[File:Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki), funcho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Summit of No. 1 Kofun}}]] [[File:Kanko-no-OKazaki-1.png|thumb|170px|The "Tatsuki Bell," once installed at the kofun summit<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://ssp.kaigiroku.net/tenant/okazaki/SpMinuteView.html?power_user=false&tenant_id=194&council_id=69&schedule_id=4&view_years=1993|title=岡崎市議会 平成5th year 6月 定例会 06月08日－12号|publisher=岡崎市議会 会議録検索システム|accessdate=2020-07-23}}</ref>.]]]]
Located in central Aichi Prefecture, the mound was built atop Kabutoyama Hill, an independent hill along the left bank of the middle reaches of the {{ill|Yahagi River|en|Yahagi River|qid=Q1132256|lt=Yahagi River|1=Yahagi River}} with an elevation of 64.5 meters. The Kabutoyama Kofun Group comprises this kofun, No. 2 Kofun (possibly part of the same structure), No. 3 Kofun (now lost), and the Mitsuiwa Kofun (also lost) {{Sfn|Yamada Nobuko|2017}}.
The hill's name, "Kabutoyama," is said to originate from local legends about {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}. Traditionally regarded as a circular Kofun (empun), it has been estimated to have a diameter of 60 meters and a height of 8 meters. Recent theories propose that it might instead be keyhole-shaped, with either No. 2 Kofun (to the south) or Kinenji Temple (to the west) forming the frontal portion of the tumulus. If true, the reconstructed mound would measure approximately 120 meters in length, making it the largest kofun in the Mikawa region {{Sfn|Yamada Nobuko|2017}}<ref group="note" name="scale">Excluding Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun, the largest kofun in the Mikawa region are the {{ill|Shōbōji Kofun|en|Shōbōji Kofun|qid=Q24867326|lt=Shōbōji Kofun|1=Shōbōji Kofun}} (94 meters, located in Nishio City) and the {{ill|Funayama No. 1 Kofun (Toyokawa)|ja|船山1号墳 (豊川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funayama No. 1 Kofun (Toyokawa)|1=Funayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q48750254}} (95 meters, located in Toyokawa City).</ref>.
Haniwa fragments (cylindrical haniwa) have been recovered from the site. Though details of the burial facility remain unclear, wooden charcoal fragments uncovered during World War II suggest a charcoal-coffin structure. Excavations have also revealed iron swords, though most finds were lost in wartime fires.
The estimated construction period is from the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}. The surrounding mounds may have been satellite tumuli.
The site was designated as a Municipal Historic Site in 1972.
== Site History == * {{ill|1944|en|1944|qid=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Showa 19): Defensive installations (observation post and air-raid shelter) were constructed atop the mound. Charcoal fragments and iron swords were uncovered during this period. * {{ill|1961|en|1961|qid=Q3696|lt=1961|1=1961}} (Showa 36): A bell tower, "Tatsuki no Kane," was installed at the mound summit<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://websv.library.okazaki.aichi.jp/winj/img/zoomify/850008252_0483.pdf|title=市政だより おかざき No.483|publisher=岡崎市役所|accessdate=2020-03-05}}</ref>. * {{ill|1971|en|1971|qid=Q2475|lt=1971|1=1971}} (Showa 46): Tatsuki no Kane began playing recorded music as a time signal<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://websv.library.okazaki.aichi.jp/winj/img/zoomify/850008252_0909.pdf|title=市政だより おかざき No.909|publisher=岡崎市役所|accessdate=2020-03-05}}</ref>. * {{ill|1972|en|1972|qid=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}} (Showa 47): Designated as a Municipal Historic Site by Okazaki City under the name "Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun."<ref name="list">{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html|title=岡崎市指定文化財目録|publisher=岡崎市ホームページ|accessdate=2017-06-02}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == === Okazaki City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun - Managed by {{ill|Kabutoyama-dera Temple|ja|甲山寺 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutoyama-dera Temple|qq=}}. Designated in 1972. Covers an area of 706.50 square meters.<ref name="list"/> ==Notes== <references group="note"/>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Literature == {{small|Sources not directly cited}} * {{Cite journal|author=Kitamura Kazuhiro|title=Reassessment of Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun - A Candidate for the Largest Keyhole Tomb in Mikawa Province|date=2009|publisher=Mikawa Archaeological Society|journal=Mikawa Archaeology|volume=20|pages=84-92}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Kabutoyama Kofun (Okazaki)}} * [https://mikawa-komachi.jp/history/kabutoyama1.html Kabutoyama No. 1 Kofun] - Mikawa Komachi, Aichi Prefecture Information Portal.
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|甲山1号墳 (岡崎市)|version=99995319|comment=Imported full ja history}}
